name: Crispy Mandarin Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 Carrots, medium, scraped and thinly sliced
2 Green peppers, medium-size, cut into 1/4-inch wide strips
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine egg white, cornstarch, and soy sauce. Mix well.
2. Add chicken pieces to the mixture, coating them thoroughly. Set aside.
3. In another bowl, mix biscuit mix, water, and sesame seeds until combined.
4. Add the chicken mixture to the biscuit mixture, stirring to ensure even coating.
5. Heat vegetable oil in a deep skillet or pot to 370 degrees Fahrenheit (190 degrees Celsius).
6. Carefully drop pieces of chicken into the hot oil one at a time.
7. Fry the chicken for 1 to 1-1/2 minutes on each side until golden brown.
8. Remove the fried chicken with tongs and drain on paper towels.
9. In a skillet, combine the juice from the Mandarin oranges with enough water to make 1 1/4 cups.
10. Bring this mixture to a boil and add the sliced carrots. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 4 to 5 minutes or until crisp-tender.
11. Add the green peppers and cook covered for an additional minute.
12. In a small bowl, combine catsup, sugar, cornstarch, vinegar, and bouillon granules. Mix well.
13. Stir this mixture into the vegetables in the skillet, returning it to a boil.
14. Add the cooked carrots, green peppers, and chicken back to the skillet.
15. Cook over medium heat until the chicken is thoroughly heated.
16. Serve hot over rice.
